payload.title: Seamless realtime sync: live local<->hosted mirror (no manual sync call)
payload.description: Today the local working copy converges with the hosted hub only when a human runs `scope sync` (idempotent one-shot). Hosted->viewer SSE and LAN gossip are already realtime; the gap is the local-copy<->hosted link. Goal: turn the one-shot into a continuous bidirectional sync agent so a laptop's .scope is a LIVE mirror of its hosted project (~100ms), while keeping the offline-first idempotent sync as the reconnect/catch-up fallback. Reuses ~80% of existing machinery: gossip.js push-on-change loop, /events SSE + Milestone-B pgBus {tenant,cursor} pointers, pullEvents(since) + persisted cursor, syncWithRemote. Build like SCP-185: sequential spine then parallel leaves. Relates SCP-185 (hosted), SCP-114 (gossip), SCP-165 (fan-out).
